title: k_thread_create return value check is wrong - NULL is never returned
description:
File: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:201-210

The code checks if k_thread_create returns NULL:
  if (tid == NULL) { ... return -ENOMEM; }

But k_thread_create() always returns a valid k_tid_t (the thread_data pointer passed in). It cannot fail at runtime - if resources are insufficient, it is a build-time config error.

This dead code:
1. Suggests a misunderstanding of the API
2. Sets running=1 before thread create, then clears on 'failure' - but the failure path never executes
3. Will cause confusion when a contributor tries to understand failure modes

Trigger: N/A (dead code)

Bad behavior: None today, but incorrect mental model leads to incorrect future changes.

Fix: Remove dead NULL check, or add comment explaining it's defensive against future API changes.
